dt-button.name: colOrder
dt-button.summary: A button collection that provides column ordering control
dt-button.description:
This button will create a collection button that when activated will show a list of the columns in the table and provide the end user with the ability to modify the ordering that is applied to the table.
The individual ordering buttons use `-button columnOrder`, and behave in the manner described in the documentation for that button - e.g. they have sub-buttons that the user can use to control the ordering direction.
While ordering of data in the table is typically done by clicking in a column header, this button type can be useful to have complete control over table ordering when columns are hidden, or if using [CardView](https://datatables.net/manual/extensions/cardview), when the table is hidden and cards are shown.
dt-button.option: The button's display text. The text can be configured using this option (see `b-init buttons.buttons.text`) or the `buttons.order.dropdown` option of the DataTables `dt-init language` object.
The button's class name. See `b-init buttons.buttons.className` for details.

Columns selector that defines the columns to include in the ordering button set. By default this is `-type undefined` which results in all columns being selected, but any of the `-type DataTable.ColumnSelector` options can be used to define a custom button set.

example.title: Show the `colOrder` button with default options
Show the `colOrder` button, but do not include the first column in the list of columns
